briefing.opening: Good morning, everyone. I will now begin the climate briefing for April 25, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Deepening Climate Crisis: A Warning to Ecosystems and Humanity
sections.content: Today's briefing begins with an alarm bell regarding the globally deepening climate crisis. Antarctica's ice kingdom is collapsing, putting penguins at risk of survival [2], while severe marine heatwaves are spreading along the coasts of California and Mexico [24]. Hot and dry climates have fueled wildfires in Georgia and Florida [17], and with global aridification, water scarcity is no longer a distant threat [33]. Research indicates that extreme weather could threaten over one-third of terrestrial animal habitats by 2085 [37], and salt marshes are also facing increased pressure due to climate change [25].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Domestic and International Policies and Governance: A Test of Transition
sections.content: Policy efforts and governance discussions to address the climate crisis are active, but simultaneously encountering difficulties. Domestically, while the Presidential Committee on Climate Crisis Response is in operation [4], concerns have been raised in parliamentary energy legislation discussions regarding excessive government influence in carbon neutrality policy decisions [5]. Internationally, while nature-related discussions at the G7 summit were successful [26, 39], there have been instances where climate change agendas were sidelined [26] or even excluded from discussions [36] to avoid conflict with the United States. Furthermore, the difficulties faced by businesses during the transition to a green economy cannot be overlooked, as exemplified by the eco-friendly and organic food specialty store 'Chorok Maeul' (Green Village), which, due to snowballing deficits, was unable to pay its suppliers, leading creditors to decide on a direct takeover [16].

sections.title: 🌱 Renewable Energy and Technological Innovation: Progress Towards a Sustainable Future
sections.content: Despite challenges, technological innovation and business expansion for renewable energy transition are steadily continuing. Domestically, the outline of a 100GW renewable energy roadmap, from reclaimed land to balcony solar panels, has been revealed [8], and a system linking SMRs (Small Modular Reactors) with renewable energy has been proposed [10]. KEPCO E&C is expanding its business into global markets by establishing a foothold for entry into Vietnam's offshore wind power market [11, 14], and Hyundai Solartech and the World Environmental Industry Education Promotion Agency are collaborating to expand solar power projects and global climate response [12]. Furthermore, Naju City is strengthening its status as an energy capital by discussing renewable energy and AI cooperation through a Korea-China forum [13], and positive progress is being observed overseas, such as a report released in Washington state indicating that clean energy is driving carbon emission reductions [42].

sections.title: 📰 Enhancing Social Awareness and Adaptation Efforts: Seeking Everyday Changes
sections.content: Efforts to raise social awareness of climate change and adapt in daily life are also underway in various ways. Olive Young is demonstrating market movement towards climate change response by launching a 'Summer Survival Items' special exhibition in preparation for the upcoming summer [1], and various domestic local governments, including Cheongsong County, Incheon Jung-gu, and Gyeongju City, are leading the spread of a carbon-neutral culture in daily life through campaigns like '10 Minutes of Rest,' lights-out events, and carbon-neutral theater productions [3, 6, 7]. Internationally, a climate change education bill has been enacted in Virginia [20], and efforts to raise awareness through education and information provision are active, such as OMSI reopening its natural science museum with a focus on climate change [22]. Furthermore, the University of Minnesota is conducting climate change-related activities using AI-powered drones [21], and research and discussions are taking place on various aspects, including the impact of climate change on the food we consume [23].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing demonstrates that even amidst the deepening climate crisis, policy discussions, technological advancements, and societal adaptation efforts are continuing across various sectors. For a sustainable future, our collective and sustained attention and action are crucial. This concludes today's climate briefing.
